PEcAn.LPJGUESS 1.9.0.9000 ← Up

Build a minimal, self-consistent cohort at a given diameter

Source: R/update.state.LPJGUESS.R
dot-seed_cohort_quick.Rd

Creates a new individual (cohort) for a given PFT using LPJ-GUESS geometric relations (Eq.5/6) at the specified diameter (typically min.diam) and an extremely small density, so that area-based pools are negligible but the cohort is immediately eligible for size-nudge.

Usage

.seed_cohort_quick(
  template,
  pft_row,
  pft_id,
  dens0 = 1e-06,
  ltor_init = 1,
  diam_cm,
  lai_indiv0 = 0.25
)

Arguments

template

A list: an existing individual used as a template (fields/slots).

pft_row

A one-row data.frame or a named numeric vector with keys: k_allom1, k_allom2, k_allom3, k_rp, crownarea_max, wooddens, sla, k_latosa.

pft_id

Integer PFT id to assign to the new individual.

dens0

Numeric, tiny density (area-scale), default 1e-6.

ltor_init

Numeric, initial leaf:root ratio to back out root mass.

diam_cm

Numeric, stem diameter in cm (use your min.diam).

lai_indiv0

Numeric, initial per-individual LAI used to back out leaf mass.

Value

A list representing the newly seeded individual with key state set (geometry/pools) and derived quantities zeroed to be recomputed by daily/allometry.

On this page
